name: Benissa, Moraira, and Calp Coastal Loop
name_original: Paseo por Calpe y Moraira – Circular desde Benissa
description: This difficult 21.7-mile touring cycling route from Benissa offers stunning coastal views, vineyards, and landmarks like the Cap Blanc Cliff
region_intro: Touring cycling around Benissa offers diverse and picturesque landscapes along Spain's Costa Blanca. The region features a network of routes winding through vineyards, hills, and along the Mediterranean coast. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, from gentle country roads to challenging ascents in areas like the Sierra de Bernia mountain range. The coastline is characterized by cliffs and coves, providing scenic views for coastal rides. Best touring cycling routes around Benissa The most popular touring cycling route is Walk through Calpe and Moraira – Circular from Benissa, a 21.7 miles (34.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route connects coastal towns, offering varied terrain and sea views. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Peñón de Ifach – Playa de la Fossa loop from Benissa, a difficult 18.3 miles (29.5 km) path. This loop provides views of the iconic Peñón de Ifach and coastal scenery. Local touring cyclists also love the Port of Moraira – Granadella Cove loop from Benissa, a 46.5 miles (74.8 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 5 hours 14 minutes. Touring cycling around Benissa is defined by coastal paths, vineyards, and mountain ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Benissa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 5000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Benissa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Benissa?
A: Benissa offers a diverse range of terrain for touring cyclists. You'll find routes winding through picturesque vineyards and rolling hills inland, as well as scenic paths along the Mediterranean coast with cliffs and coves. The region provides everything from gentle country roads suitable for leisurely rides to challenging ascents in areas like the Sierra de Bernia mountain range.

Q: Are there any challenging climbs for experienced touring cyclists in Benissa?
A: Yes, Benissa is known for its challenging climbs, attracting even professional cyclists. A prominent feature is the &lt;strong&gt;Coll de Rates&lt;/strong&gt;, an iconic climb spanning 7.1 kilometers with an average gradient of 5.45%, offering stunning sea views from the top. The Sierra de Bernia mountain range also provides demanding routes with panoramic vistas.

Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Benissa?
A: There are over 45 touring cycling routes around Benissa documented on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, with options ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every cyclist.

Q: What are some popular coastal touring cycling routes near Benissa?
A: Many touring cycling routes in Benissa offer beautiful coastal views. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952010068/walk-through-calpe-and-moraira-circular-from-benissa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walk through Calpe and Moraira – Circular from Benissa&lt;/a&gt; connects coastal towns, providing varied terrain and sea vistas. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20474251" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Peñón de Ifach – Playa de la Fossa loop from Benissa&lt;/a&gt;, which offers views of the iconic Peñón de Ifach.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Benissa?
A: Yes, Benissa offers several easy touring cycling routes suitable for families. There are 5 easy routes available, often featuring gentle country roads through vineyards and rural countryside, providing a pleasant experience for all ages. Look for routes with lower elevation gains and shorter distances for a comfortable family ride.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Benissa's touring cycling routes pass by a variety of natural features and landmarks. You can explore the picturesque &lt;strong&gt;Jalón Valley&lt;/strong&gt; with its vineyards and charming villages, or ride along the stunning Mediterranean coastline with its cliffs and coves. Some routes may even offer views of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/889730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Calpe&lt;/a&gt;, where you might spot flamingos.

Q: Can I find routes that pass through traditional Spanish villages?
A: Absolutely. Many touring cycling routes in Benissa venture inland, passing through or connecting charming traditional villages such as Senija, Parcent, Murla, and Gata de Gorgos. These offer a glimpse into local culture and provide opportunities for breaks and refreshments.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Benissa?
A: The touring cycling routes in Benissa are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from coastal paths to inland vineyards and challenging mountain ranges, as well as the well-maintained network of routes suitable for various skill levels.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of historical sites or castles?
A: Yes, some touring cycling routes in the Benissa region provide views of historical sites. For example, you might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/793288" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moraira Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2601737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Granadella Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of scenic beauty and cultural interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6680697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church-Fortress of Saint Michael the Archangel&lt;/a&gt; in Murla is another notable historical landmark.

Q: Are there any loop routes for touring cyclists in Benissa?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Benissa are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39557154" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Les Bassetes – Port of Moraira loop from Restaurante Asia&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39559740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Les Bassetes – Peñón de Ifach loop from Ebike Hire&lt;/a&gt;, offering convenient circular journeys.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Benissa?
A: Benissa benefits from an agreeable climate, making it suitable for touring cycling for much of the year. The spring and autumn months generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer while still providing plenty of sunshine. Winter can also be pleasant, especially for those escaping colder climates.
tips:
The loop around the lake is recommended. It's easy to navigate. There are small observation points where you can take good photos.

The Calpe salt flats were used for salt production as early as Roman times and remained in operation until the 20th century. Today, they are a protected nature reserve and provide a protected habitat for numerous animal species. They are particularly known for their flamingos and other waterfowl, making them an ideal spot for birdwatchers. Paths around the salt flats offer impressive views of the Peñón de Ifach.
Very nice view of the (bay) Cala Les Bassetes. Crystal clear water. Top location. Bathing is possible, the toilets and a shower are publicly accessible. Well maintained! Absolutely recommended!👍

Wonderful views along the cliffs. Easily accessible via a paved path. At the end of the small road, you can also take a staircase down to the water. Don't be put off by the many houses. Here you have a wide view over the coast and the glittering Mediterranean. The steep cliffs fall into the sea, while the water shines in various shades of blue and turquoise.

A very popular photo motif, almost everyone can be photographed here. Moraira is basically worthwhile as a destination for a tour, a very beautiful town. If you have the opportunity, you should spend an evening there on the rocky coast. Really particularly beautiful, will be remembered.

faqs.question: Is the Walk through Calpe and Moraira – Circular from Benissa suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this touring cycling route is rated as difficult and requires very good fitness. It covers 34.9 km with an elevation gain of over 520 meters, making it challenging for inexperienced cyclists.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features varied terrain, including coastal paths, hills, and areas with vineyards. While it has mostly paved surfaces, some segments may be unpaved and challenging. You'll encounter a mix of road, street, cycleway, singletrack, path, and access road surfaces.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque and diverse landscapes, including stunning sea views, coastal cliffs, and coves. The route offers views of the iconic Peñón de Ifach, passes through natural areas with pine forests, and provides glimpses of vineyards and the Sierra de Bernia mountain range. Keep an eye out for flamingos at the Salinas de Calpe.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or highlights will I see on the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass several interesting points. These include the Salinas de Calpe, Cala Les Bassetes, the Cap Blanc Cliffs, Moraira Castle, and the Ermita de la Font Santa.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Costa Blanca region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, with milder temperatures. Summer can be very hot, so early morning rides are recommended if cycling during this season. Winter is also a good option, as temperatures remain relatively mild compared to other regions.

faqs.question: Where does the route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route starting and ending in Benissa. While specific parking details aren't provided, Benissa offers various public parking options within the town. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the route itself doesn't have specific restrictions against dogs, it is primarily a touring cycling route. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure it is safely secured in a bike trailer or carrier and that you adhere to local leash laws and etiquette, especially in populated areas or protected natural zones.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Walk through Calpe and Moraira – Circular from Benissa. It uses public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will cycle along parts of the Paseo ecologico de Besnissa, Carrer dels Berbena, Carrer de la Perera, Camí de les Cases de Sardi, Carrer del Tender, Camí dels Trons, and Paso subterraneo.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route connects coastal towns like Calpe and Moraira, you'll find numerous opportunities for refreshments. Both towns, as well as Benissa, offer a wide selection of cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and length, it's essential to be well-prepared. Bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bicycle, appropriate cycling attire, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and a fully charged phone. A helmet is highly recommended for safety.
